title: Heatwave intensifies across Odisha, Jharsuguda sizzles at 44.8 deg C - Odisha TV

full_text:
Heatwave conditions continue to intensify across Odisha, with several districts in the western region recording extreme temperatures and severe discomfort. The situation worsened steadily, with hot and dry winds affecting both interior and coastal areas.
Jharsuguda emerged as the hottest place in the state on Friday, recording a maximum temperature of 44.8 degrees Celsius. Titlagarh recorded 44 degrees Celsius, while Sambalpur registered 43.6 degrees Celsius. Districts including Bolangir and Sundargarh are also witnessing intense heat, contributing to rising concerns over public health and daily activities.
Warnings Issued For Multiple Districts
The Meteorological Department issued heatwave warnings for Jharsuguda, Sundargarh, Bargarh, Bolangir and Sambalpur, indicating that high temperatures are likely to persist. Authorities advised residents in these districts to remain cautious and take necessary precautions.
In addition to the heatwave alerts, hot and humid conditions are expected to prevail in several parts of the state. Alerts were issued for 13 districts, Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Jagatsinghpur, Cuttack, Khordha, Puri, Nayagarh, Ganjam and Gajapati, where discomfort levels are likely to remain high.
Early Summer Vacation Announced
Amid the prevailing heatwave conditions, the state government has announced early summer vacation for schools from April 27. The decision was taken as a precautionary measure to safeguard students from the adverse effects of extreme heat.
With temperature remaining high and no immediate relief in sight, authorities continue to monitor the situation closely while urging the public to limit outdoor exposure during peak hours.
